data.title: ZSDynamics 1.1
data.name: ZSDynamics 1.1
data.subtitle: Built with Flask
data.summary: After digging deeper into data science, I wanted the portfolio to be more than a place to drop my work. So I added a simple email subscription so visitors could get updates, and I integrated Dashly for some lightweight analytics and visualization. It was a noticeable step forward for me—not just in the code, but in how I was thinking about the people visiting the site.
data.oneLiner: This version turned the portfolio from a static project list into an interactive site with email subscriptions and built-in analytics to better understand user engagement.

readme.content:
# ZSDynamics Static Portfolio

This is a static version of the ZSDynamics portfolio website.

## Running the Site

To serve this site locally:

```bash
npx serve docs
```

Or if you have serve installed globally:

```bash
serve docs
```

Then open your browser to the URL shown in the terminal (usually http://localhost:3000).

## Structure

- `index.html` - Home page
- `/about/` - About page
- `/portfolio/` - Portfolio page  
- `/data_analytics/` - Data Analytics page
- `/back_end/` - Back-end Developer page
- `/front_end/` - Front-end Developer page
- `/contact/` - Contact page
- `/sliding_scale_project_page/` - Sliding Scale Project page
- `/battle_fictional_character/` - Character Battle page
- `/periodic_table/` - Periodic Table page
- `/static/` - Static assets (CSS, JS, images)
- `/dashboard_data/` - Data files for dashboard
- `/dash_app/` - Dash application (if available)

## Note

This static version removes server-side functionality like:
- Email sending
- Form submissions  
- Database interactions
- Session management

The contact forms and subscription forms are preserved for styling but will not function.
